title: Manifold learning: t-SNE and UMAP
key_concepts: Linear methods fail on complex, non-linear manifolds (e.g., Swiss roll dataset).
t-SNE focuses on preserving local neighborhood structure (points that are close remain close) but often distorts global distances and is computationally slow.
UMAP (Uniform Manifold Approximation and Projection) is the modern academic primary. It balances local and global structure better, is significantly faster, and rests on Riemannian geometry.
Both are primarily for visualization, not generally recommended as preprocessing for downstream models.
equations: Both methods minimize divergence between high-dimensional similarities $p_{i|j}$ and low-dimensional similarities $q_{i|j}$ (e.g., Kullback-Leibler divergence for t-SNE: $KL(P || Q)$).
examples: Visualizing thousands of high-dimensional single-cell RNA sequences or image embeddings in 2D.
relations: Contrast with PCA (linear vs non-linear). Emphasize UMAP as the preferred modern tool over t-SNE.
